Ad Text:
A high-growth FinTech is hiring a FP&A Manager in a newly created CBD-based role following recent expansion.
This is a hands-on, high-impact position responsible for building FP&A capability from scratch, partnering with leadership, and driving commercial decision-making across the business.
Responsibilities
Build and own financial models to support strategic decisions, new products, and investment cases
Deliver scenario analysis to guide pricing, growth strategy, and business planning
Analyse product performance, pricing, and unit economics to drive profitability insights
Lead budgeting, forecasting, and long-range planning
Establish and improve FP&A frameworks, reporting, and performance metrics from the ground up
Partner closely with the CFO and senior stakeholders to deliver clear, actionable insights
Support commercial decision-making across product, tech, and operations
Requirements
Proven FP&A experience in a high-growth / fast-paced environment (FinTech, tech, financial services preferably)
Strong financial modelling skills — confident building models from scratch
Experience with new products, pricing analysis, or revenue modelling
Track record of setting up or improving processes and reporting frameworks
Highly commercial mindset — able to translate data into business insights
Self-starter with a proactive, hands-on approach
Strong communication skills and ability to partner with senior stakeholders
Why Apply?
Work closely with CFO and leadership team on key business decisions
Own and build FP&A capability end-to-end
High commercial exposure across pricing, product, and performance
Opportunity to step up as the finance function scales

Business Analyst 

Client: Sydney Trains (Suburban Services Branch)

JOB REF: REQ 24029

Start Date: ASAP, preferably someone who is immediately available, or on 2 weeks’ notice, maximum

Reports into: Senior Manager Workforce Development & Programs

Initial End Date: 6 months, with possible extension

Maximum Rate: $900 to $990 per day, plus super (Circa $1,108.80 per day, plus GST if an ABN)

Location: 100% In-Office role; currently in Burwood office but moving to Central (Eddy Ave) Office.

 

Sydney Trains provides train services throughout Greater Sydney region and was established in July 2013.  Its vision is to “keep Sydney Moving by putting the customer at the centre of everything it does and delivering safe, reliable and clean rail services to the people of Sydney”.  The organisation is focused on providing sustainable, efficient and cost-effective services to the passengers of the Suburban and Intercity rail network. The Sydney Trains Suburban Services branch are the proud custodians of their customers’ end-to-end journey and overall experience. The branch includes train crew, station staff, station cleaners, and transport officers as their key frontline staff. The branch is actively working through initiatives to improve their operational response and resilience during service disruptions. 

They have a number of initiatives that need driving, Process, People, and Technology, to improve the services for the passengers. The Business Analyst leads the gathering and analysis of business and technical requirements to ensure business problems are thoroughly understood, proposed solutions are fit for purpose and human-centered, and to support decision making and planning for Suburban Services initiatives. Responsibilities include:

Deliver high quality stakeholder-informed business analytics, research, insights, and reporting, with consideration for relevant business rules, processes, procedures, and service guidelines.

Define people, process, and technology business requirements to ensure that initiatives deliver intended business outcomes.

Assist project managers and business with cost-benefit analysis and prioritisation.

Ensure that the delivery matches the outcomes and is fit-for-purpose.

Assist with the development of business cases, financial models and budget submissions to secure timely funding for projects.

Prepare quality reports and presentations for use by the SSB management and stakeholders.

Required Skills and Experience (Selection Criteria):

8 Years+ Business Analyst experience.

Demonstrated experience as a business analyst including research, planning, development of functional and technical specifications and business case, and data analysis methodologies in a complex operational environment.

Must have in-depth experience in Human-Centered approaches, and preferably Human-Centered Design.

They use Double Diamond, a foundational framework for Human-Centered Design, which ensures that solutions are rooted in deep empathy for user needs, rather than technological feasibility or business goals alone. Any experience within this is highly advantageous.

An understanding of technology and legacy systems e.g., moving across to paperless systems (been adopted a lot within Electricity industry of late), managing rosters, workforce management etc. Any experience with Power BI and dashboard reporting will be of interest.

Strong stakeholder management, must establish credibility with frontline users.

Demonstrated experience in project lifecycles, and problem solving.

Demonstrated experience in reports and presentations.

They need someone with a deep connection and understanding of the customer and the staff providing the services.

Someone with a Transport background is preferable e.g., Logistics, Freight (e.g., truck drivers), Airlines, Transport NSW, Sydney Trains, RMS, Ferries etc.

Qualification in a tertiary field of study with core knowledge relating to business analysis, statistics, or methods.

 


PLEASE NOTE: Please only apply if you meet all the selection criteria (and this is demonstrated clearly in your resume).


Due to the high volume of responses expected, we will only reply if your application is successful!

About Us

At Sportsbet, we’re united behind our purpose to “Bring Excitement to Life” for our 2 million customers (and growing!). We do this by over-delivering through our commitment to fun, challenging the status quo and living our values. As part of the Flutter Group, we’re a global player, but in Melbourne, Sydney and Darwin we’re a tight knit group of 1,300 teammates (of which over half are in our Tech and Data Science teams!).

We are a significant contributor to the Australian economy through racing and sporting codes and we are committed to having a positive impact in society by supporting our customers, our communities and our employees. Our commitment to responsible gambling is genuine and demonstrated through the wide range of responsible gambling initiatives and tools. 

In 2023, we were a recipient of LinkedIn’s Top 25 Companies for our focus on career development and awarded the Top 10 Workplaces for Women through Work180. Our commitment to making Sportsbet the best place to work is never-ending. 

Sportsbet is Australia’s leading online sports and racing wagering brand — and part of Flutter Entertainment, a FTSE100 and NYSE-listed global powerhouse. We operate in a fast-moving, highly regulated environment where great strategy really matters.

We’re looking for a Senior Strategy Manager to join us. This is a high-impact role for someone who loves solving complex problems, influencing senior leaders, and turning insight into action, all while working in a collaborative, supportive, and ambitious team.

The Opportunity

As a Strategy Manager, you’ll play a hands-on role in shaping Sportsbet’s future. You’ll work closely with our executive leaders, including the CEO, GMs and international Flutter teams, to frame the questions that matter most and deliver strategies that drive real outcomes.

You’ll balance big-picture thinking with practical delivery, bringing structure, clarity and momentum to some of the most important initiatives across the business.

What You’ll Be Doing

Shape the Strategy

Help develop Sportsbet’s long-term company strategy and annual OKRs
Bring fresh thinking and structured problem-solving to our biggest opportunities and challenges

Lead Meaningful Projects

Own the end-to-end delivery of priority strategic projects — from framing and analysis through to recommendations and execution
Combine quantitative analysis with customer, market and qualitative insights to surface what really matters
Translate complexity into clear, compelling narratives that influence decisions

Collaborate & Lead

Work across diverse, cross-functional teams (strategy, analytics, finance, tech, product and more)
Step into an emerging leadership role on projects, building alignment and momentum across the organisation
Partner closely with the Strategy & Research team to deliver work that’s focused, thoughtful and sustainable

About You

Tertiary qualification in business, finance, economics, STEM or similar (MBA is highly valued)
3-5 years of experience in top-tier management consulting, investment banking, corporate strategy and/or a fast based strategic leadership role in a high growth environment
Strong strategic and analytical capability, with confidence working across data, insights and financials to inform decisions
A collaborative influencer who builds trust with stakeholders and drives alignment across diverse teams, including senior leaders
An excellent communicator who can simplify complex problems, tell a compelling story, and turn insight into action
Curious, adaptable and commercially minded, with a genuine interest in tech, direct-to-consumer businesses and/or sports and wagering

The Perks

We work hard and play hard, so along with a competitive salary and generous performance-based bonus, here’s some of our other perks:

25 days annual leave (that’s right, a whole extra week!)
Genuine flexible working policy with a $800 work from home office allowance
Weekly concierge service including free massages, manicure/nail appointments – all onsite
Development budget to send you to conferences, events, courses and other learning opportunities.
End of trip facilities with an onsite table tennis and pool table
Access to our Employee Assistance Program 
Industry leading Parental Leave support program with 26 weeks paid leave for Primary Carers and no wait time. We also have a progressive return to work benefit for your first 6 months back: come back for 3 days, get paid for 4; come back for 4 days, get paid for 5.

We encourage you to apply as soon as possible as we review candidates within 2 weeks of advertising. Be sure to check out our Life@Sportsbet Instagram page, Sportsbet LinkedIn page as well our careers website for more information and insight into our culture.

Job Title: Managing Director - AI & Cloud $400K-$500k base, super, bonus
Ad Text:
Following sustained growth and continued investment in AI and cloud across ANZ, a global consulting leader is expanding its Google Business Group and has created a mandate for a Managing Director – AI & Cloud to drive enterprise transformation at scale.

This is a front-of-market origination role, focused on opening strategic conversations, shaping transformation agendas, and activating complex programs in partnership with Google Cloud.

You will engage directly with executive stakeholders to redefine how organisations operate in an AI-first world, moving beyond technology adoption into true business reinvention.

Why This Role Stands Out
Create, don’t inherit – pure origination focus with autonomy to build strategic pipeline
Strategic alignment with Google Cloud across AI, data and cloud platforms
Operate at C-suite and board level across ANZ enterprise
Work within a fully integrated model – strategy through to execution
Influence high-value, multi-year transformation programs
Direct exposure to ANZ and global leadership
Be part of a growth engine where AI is central to every client conversation
What You’ll Be Responsible For
Originate and shape complex enterprise transformation opportunities
Lead senior client conversations across AI, cloud and operating model change
Frame business-led value propositions, linking technology to measurable outcomes
Build and expand joint opportunities with Google Cloud stakeholders
Qualify and structure early-stage deals, guiding them through to execution
Mobilise multi-disciplinary teams to support pursuit strategy and delivery alignment
About the Organisation

A global leader in enterprise transformation, bringing together strategy, consulting, technology, and operations into a single integrated model designed for the Age of AI.
The Google Business Group is a core strategic pillar, combining deep industry expertise with leading AI and cloud capabilities to help organisations unlock value at scale.

What You Bring
Track record of originating and shaping large, complex enterprise deals
Background in consulting, advisory, or enterprise technology environments
Established ANZ enterprise network (Sydney/Melbourne)
Strong credibility with C-suite and senior decision-makers
Ability to connect AI and cloud capability to business transformation outcomes
Commercial mindset with a focus on growth, influence, and execution
This is a rare opportunity to step into a true market-defining role, shaping how AI and cloud are applied across enterprise organisations in ANZ.

Ad Text:
Work.

12.75% superannuation

Water.

Your new team

At Auto & General (A&G), we're a rapidly growing insurance business committed to safeguarding a brighter future for our customers, people, and community. We foster a high-performance culture built on integrity, collaboration, and dedication, known as the A&G Way. As an individual contributor in our Group Risk, Operational Risk division, you'll be part of a team that champions effective data management, governance, and quality practices across the organisation. As a leader, you'll model our values, drive high performance, and embody a One Team mindset.

The Role:
We're seeking a Senior Manager, Data Risk to provide crucial Line 2 oversight and challenge to data management, governance, and quality practices across the Auto & General Group. Based in Toowong or Melbourne, you will ensure our data-related risk controls align with regulatory, legislative, and industry best practices. This individual contributor role involves partnering with data owners, technology teams, and governance functions to enhance enterprise-wide practices, uplift our data strategy, and ensure regulatory compliance, ultimately supporting broader business objectives.


Position Accountabilities 

As Senior Manager, Data Risk, you will be accountable for:

Line 2 Risk Oversight:

Providing independent oversight and effective challenge to data risk management practices and programs.
Monitoring adherence to enterprise-wide data governance, quality standards, frameworks, and controls.
Reviewing and assessing material data risk exposure across Line 1 initiatives, including transformation projects, cloud migrations, and third-party arrangements.
Advising on risk implications of AGIC's data strategies, ensuring alignment with enterprise risk framework and business objectives.
Data Governance Frameworks:

Leading the embedding of the enterprise data risk framework, aligned with regulatory requirements and industry best practices.
Partnering with Line 1 to promote consistent application of data ownership models, metadata management practices, and stewardship frameworks.
Ensuring data risk management practices align with regulatory and legislative requirements, and internal policies.
Supporting regulatory reviews and audits, advising on data governance, reporting, and risk alignment for third-party data services.
Risk Assessments, Reporting and Insights:

Conducting thematic reviews and deep dives into critical data areas.
Providing detailed insights on emerging risks, ensuring these are reflected in risk profiles and action plans.
Delivering high-quality insights and commentary on data risk exposures, control effectiveness, and remediation progress to senior management and governance committees.
Tracking and reporting on risk issues, control gaps, and risk acceptances, providing metrics that measure the impact and progress of data governance efforts.

 

Qualifications & Experience:

7-10 years of experience in enterprise data governance and data risk, preferably within a regulated financial services or insurance environment.
Proven experience in Line 2 risk, assurance, or internal audit functions is highly desirable.
Expertise in data lifecycle management, data architecture principles, and regulatory compliance obligations.
High-level leadership and engagement skills, with the ability to manage SME experts the A&G way.
Excellent communication, influencing, and negotiation skills.
Ability to build effective relationships with senior managers and other key stakeholders.
Experience in the general insurance industry is highly regarded.
Qualifications:
Tertiary qualifications in Information Management, Data Governance, or related disciplines.
Professional certifications preferred: CDMP, CIMP, or related data governance and risk credentials.

Our Perks 


Location – Toowong

Close to a variety of public transport and easy parking options, the Toowong office has excellent end of trip facilities (including secure bike storage and showers), outdoor spaces and plenty of modern collaborative areas to work and connect with colleagues. 

Conveniently based at Toowong Village tower with views of the mountains and the Brisbane River, our location is only 4 km from the city and provides access to retail, restaurants and other amenities.

 


The benefits of working at A&G

Be rewarded - we recognise high performance and reward our people for their hard work through bonuses and other perks.
Work flexibility - with options to work from home two days per week.
Options for leave - life happens, so we’ve got volunteer days, an additional paid ‘ME’ day, paid parental leave and the opportunity to purchase additional leave to cover all of the big stuff.
Grow with us - we’ve got learning and professional development opportunities to suit everyone. 
Give back - our A&G Difference program gives you the power to change our community for the better through volunteering, fundraising and donation opportunities for causes that you’re passionate about.
Take care of yourself - your wellbeing is important to us and our healthy mind and body hub, mental-health support and fitness discounts will help you be your best self.
Celebrate the wins - we love sharing our successes and celebrating together - join us and you’ve got a ticket to our many on-site events throughout the year, family fun days and annual celebrations.
Save money - as well as discounts on insurance products, we’ve teamed up with some incredible retailers, hospitality providers and others to bring you discounts on your purchases, no matter where you are in Australia.

About us

Auto & General (A&G) is the fastest-growing major Motor and Home insurer in Australia, providing insurance products and solutions to safeguard a brighter future for our customers and community.  

Our range of general insurance products including Car, Motorcycle, Home, Contents Pet and Travel products are delivered through our multi-award-winning brand Budget Direct and partnerships with leading brands - ING, Qantas, and Coles Insurance.

We’re excited about the future and we’re always on the lookout for talented, passionate individuals who can help us achieve our goal of being Australia’s best insurer! If this sounds like you, apply today.


Auto & General values individual differences and believes in fostering an inclusive culture that creates a great place to work for all. 

*A note from Auto & General to recruitment agencies: We politely ask that you avoid making any approaches or sending any unsolicited resumes to our Recruitment Team or Hiring Leaders across our business. Auto & General is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

About the Job 

Due to the continued success and growth of Seven Consulting, we are committed to building a Change Management Practice to complement our Program Delivery, Program Management Office (PMO) and Consulting services.  

Established in 2002, Seven Consulting is Australia's best program delivery company. Our results show client satisfaction at 99.25% and team satisfaction at 98.68% for 2025, and those figures continue to hold firm. 

We specialise in the successful delivery of large-scale, complex IT-enabled business transformation projects ranging from $1M to $1.4Bn with some of Australia's top companies. We have offices in Sydney, Melbourne and Manila. 

We are looking for talented Change Analysts/Associates with relevant experience, who share our passion for delivering quality outcomes through business change, to join our team on a full-time basis. 




Your New Role 

As a Change Analyst/Associate, you will work directly with our clients across all areas of Change Management to deliver high quality outcomes. As this is a junior role, you will work closely with more senior team members on client engagements. Each engagement is different, but typical responsibilities of a Change Analyst/Associate include: 

Analysing a client's business particularly where there is a potential impact from change 

Meeting and presenting to stakeholders including preparing business documents and slides  

Proposing appropriate tactics to manage organisational changes for a project in a way that leads to the desired outcomes 

Executing on plans, scheduling, reporting on progress, raising risks and issues, maintaining effective relationships, and being a supportive project team member 

Applying past experience in projects to demonstrate knowledge of Project Management principles as well as Agile, Waterfall and blended methods 

Providing the needed level of support to the recipients of change during and after the project 




What You Bring 

Coming into the Change Management Practice at the Change Analyst/Associate level means that you will bring professional experience in consulting and/or knowledge of project delivery and exposure to delivery of organisational change. Typically, Change Analysts/Associates have: 

1 to 2 years of experience in Change Management, Project Delivery and/or PMO roles 

A relevant certification or a Bachelor's degree (often in Psychology and Organisational Psychology, Communications/Journalism, Human Resources, Project Management, Applied / Behavioural Science or similar) 

Proficiency in the Microsoft Office Suite, especially Word and PowerPoint  

Strong written and verbal communication skills 

A desire and ability to contribute to the Change Management Practice




You will also possess the following essential attributes for managing change at this level 

Resilience 

An inquisitive nature 

Empathy  

Problem analysis and solving 

To be eligible for employment you must either be an Australian citizen or permanent resident or have a visa with work rights. 




What We Offer 

Seven Consulting is committed to building a community of high calibre Program Delivery, PMO, Change Management and Consulting professionals and providing a rewarding career by offering: 

A continuous learning and development environment 

In-house training sessions and external training allowances 

Individual career development plans  

A mentoring / mentee program 

A collaborative and inclusive culture 

Excellent remuneration, regular and well-defined bonuses and profit share 

Paid overtime 

Recognition and rewards for high performance 

Job security and support during personal hardships 

A team charity initiative – Project 7 – to support the community 

Quarterly social and family events 

Please visit our website to learn more careers and culture at Seven: https://www.sevenconsulting.com/ 




Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion 

Seven Consulting embraces diversity and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, colour, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin or on the basis of disability. 

Seven Consulting is committed to providing a safe environment for our team members. All applicants are required to provide any relevant checks and COVID immunisation certificates. 

This is an excellent opportunity to join Seven and be part of our continued growth and success. If you are looking for an organisation that values your experience and expertise, then apply now to be involved in some of the industry’s most significant projects. 

Please apply online or contact our HR team on (03) 9617 8200 for a confidential discussion.  




Our Team Satisfaction Results: https://www.sevenconsulting.com/team-satisfaction-survey/

Exceptional interpersonal, communication and presentation skills.

Job Title: Manager, Portfolio Oversight, Fixed Income, Currency & Credit

Ad Text:
The role

As our Manager Technology Assurance, you’ll be at the forefront of ensuring that Auto & General’s technology systems and digital platforms operate safely, securely, and effectively. You will lead independent assurance reviews across our technology landscape, assessing the effectiveness of IT controls, risk management practices, and emerging technology initiatives. Partnering with a team of Assurance Analysts, you’ll provide strategic insights that shape technology risk decisions, strengthen compliance, and support senior leadership in safeguarding the organisation’s digital environment. This is a pivotal role where your expertise will directly influence the resilience and reliability of our technology systems.


Position Accountabilities

In this role, you will:

Lead Technology Assurance Programs: Oversee and deliver the annual assurance plan with a focus on technology systems, digital platforms, and emerging technologies, ensuring IT controls are robust and effective.
Drive Regulatory & Policy Compliance: Ensure technology operations comply with internal policies and external regulations (APRA, ASIC), collaborating closely with the Compliance and IT teams to resolve issues.
Provide Strategic Technology Insights: Deliver actionable assurance reports and recommendations on technology risk and control effectiveness, influencing senior leadership and committee decisions.
Foster Stakeholder Collaboration: Build strong relationships with executives, technology leaders, and key stakeholders to embed a proactive technology risk culture.
Champion Innovation in Assurance: Continuously improve technology assurance methodologies, leveraging new tools and techniques to enhance efficiency, coverage, and insight.
Lead and Develop the Assurance Team: Mentor and guide Assurance Analysts in conducting technology-focused reviews, control testing, and risk assessments, ensuring high-quality outcomes.
Drive Strategic Tech Initiatives: Lead cross-functional projects related to operational and technology risk, providing assurance input for digital transformation, IT programs, and emerging technology adoption.

 

What experience you’ll bring

Proven Technology Risk Expertise: 5+ years in risk, assurance, audit, or compliance, including 2–3 years in 2LOD technology assurance or IT risk.
Technical & Regulatory Knowledge: Strong understanding of IT controls, risk frameworks (COSO, ISO 31000), and regulations (APRA CPS 220/230/234, ASIC RG 271).
Analytical & Strategic Thinking: Ability to translate complex technology risks into clear, actionable insights.
Qualifications: Bachelor’s in Technology, Finance, Business, Risk, or Law; professional certifications (CISA, CIA, CRISC, CPA) highly desirable.
Stakeholder & Communication Skills: Able to influence and present confidently to executive-level stakeholders.
Leadership Skills: Experience leading and developing high-performing assurance or risk teams.
Demonstrated expertise: in designing, executing, and overseeing complex risk assessments, control testing, and assurance reviews. 
Thorough understanding: of the Technology Landscape, especially emerging technologies with a risk lens.

 

Location –Toowong

Close to a variety of public transport and easy parking options, the Toowong office has excellent end of trip facilities (including secure bike storage and showers), outdoor spaces and plenty of modern collaborative areas to work and connect with colleagues. 

Conveniently based at Toowong Village tower with views of the mountains and the Brisbane River, our location is only 4 km from the city and provides access to retail, restaurants and other amenities.

 

The benefits of working at A&G

Be rewarded - we recognise high performance and reward our people for their hard work through bonuses and other perks.
Work flexibility - with options to work from home up to two days per week.
Options for leave - life happens, so we’ve got volunteer days, an additional paid ‘ME’ day, paid parental leave and the opportunity to purchase additional leave to cover all of the big stuff.
Grow with us - we’ve got learning and professional development opportunities to suit everyone.
Give back - our A&G Difference program gives you the power to change our community for the better through volunteering, fundraising and donation opportunities for causes that you’re passionate about.
Take care of yourself - your wellbeing is important to us and our healthy mind and body hub, mental-health support and fitness discounts will help you be your best self.
Celebrate the wins - we love sharing our successes and celebrating together - join us and you’ve got a ticket to our many on-site events throughout the year, family fun days and annual celebrations.
Save money - as well as discounts on insurance products, we’ve teamed up with some incredible retailers, hospitality providers and others to bring you discounts on your purchases, no matter where you are in Australia.

 

About us

Auto & General (A&G) is the fastest-growing major Motor and Home insurer in Australia, providing insurance products and solutions to safeguard a brighter future for our customers and community.  

Our range of general insurance products including Car, Motorcycle, Home, Contents Pet and Travel products are delivered through our multi-award-winning brand Budget Direct and partnerships with leading brands - ING, Qantas, and Coles Insurance.

We’re excited about the future and we’re always on the lookout for talented, passionate individuals who can help us achieve our goal of being Australia’s best insurer! If this sounds like you, apply today.

 

Auto & General values individual differences and believes in fostering an inclusive culture that creates a great place to work for all.

About the Role

You’ll play a key role in validating data loads and transformations between strategic data collection platforms and legacy data warehouses, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and reliability across the data ecosystem.




This is a hands‑on role suited to someone who is technically strong, highly detail‑oriented, and comfortable working closely with engineers and data specialists.




Key Responsibilities

Develop and execute test plans, test cases, and test scripts for data platform components, including ETL/ELT processes and data pipelines
Validate data migrations and transformations from legacy on‑premise systems (e.g. SQL Server, Oracle) to target data warehouses
Perform data reconciliation between source and target systems to ensure completeness, consistency, and accuracy
Execute data testing using SQL and/or Python to extract, compare, and validate large datasets
Investigate, document, and track defects using tools such as JIRA
Liaise with data engineers, architects, and delivery teams to support defect resolution and root cause analysis
Support User Acceptance Testing (UAT) by preparing data scenarios, guiding users, and capturing feedback
Ensure adherence to testing standards and contribute to the evolution of best‑practice testing approaches
Nature of the Work
Embedded within a large‑scale data transformation program
Hands‑on, technically focused testing across data pipelines and platforms
Strong collaboration with data engineers, architects, and senior technical specialists
Mix of:
Data validation and reconciliation
Defect investigation and reporting
Stakeholder collaboration across technology and data teams
What You’ll Bring
3+ years’ experience in a QA or data testing role, ideally within data migration or data platform projects
Strong SQL skills for data validation, reconciliation, and transformation testing
Solid understanding of ETL/ELT frameworks and data pipelines
Excellent attention to detail with strong problem‑solving capability
Clear written and verbal communication skills to engage cross‑functionally
Desirable Experience
Python or PySpark experience for automated data testing
Exposure to agile delivery, DevOps, or DataOps environments
Experience within financial services, regulatory reporting, or APRA collections

Job Title: Senior Data Analyst, External Reporting - 6 Month Fixed Term Contract
Ad Text:
ING Australia are on the hunt for an experienced Senior Data Analyst – External Reporting to join our Finance team in Sydney on a 6 month fixed term contract.

As Senior Analyst – External Reporting, you’ll sit at the intersection of finance, data, and regulation, ensuring our external and prudential reports are accurate, compliant, and future ready. This is a hands-on role where you’ll deep‑dive into complex data, review and validate SQL and SSIS code, and translate regulatory requirements into robust reporting solutions.

You’ll work closely with IT, the Data Tribe, and the Finance Transformation team to uplift reporting processes, strengthen controls, and drive automation. Beyond traditional reporting, you’ll also contribute to the transformation of raw data into meaningful insights, including emerging sustainability and regulatory disclosures. If you enjoy combining technical expertise with regulatory interpretation and continuous improvement, this role offers the opportunity to make a real impact in a highly visible area of the bank.

We’re looking for a technically strong and detail‑driven analyst with a passion for data integrity, regulatory interpretation, and continuous improvement, who can bridge finance, technology, and regulation.


Ready to make an impact?
 

What you’ll do  

Review and validate SQL database code and SSIS packages used in liquidity and external reporting to ensure compliance with APRA prudential standards and Group guidelines
Support delivery of Liquidity Reporting (including ARS 210) across ING Bank
Identify, investigate, and resolve data integrity issues and reporting discrepancies, ensuring accuracy and consistency of reporting outputs
Develop and maintain high‑quality technical and regulatory documentation, including interpretation registers, position papers, and data flow documentation
Partner with IT, Data Tribe, and Finance Transformation teams to drive automation and process improvements, reducing manual reporting effort
Contribute to the development of centralised data repositories and automated reporting solutions
Support ad‑hoc regulatory and industry initiatives, including LCR/NSFR inputs for industry‑wide stress testing
Act as a key point of contact for troubleshooting reporting issues and supporting audit and review activities

What we’re looking for

Tertiary qualification in Finance, Computer Science, Data Analytics, Actuarial Studies, or a related discipline with a strong analytical or numerical focus
5+ years’ experience in financial services, ideally in external, regulatory, or prudential reporting roles
Strong technical skills in MS SQL Server, SSIS, and Visual Studio, with proven experience reviewing complex codebases
Ability to interpret regulatory requirements and translate them into robust technical and reporting solutions
Excellent analytical skills with a high level of accuracy and attention to detail
Strong written and verbal communication skills, able to clearly explain technical and regulatory concepts
Experience with APRA prudential standards, particularly liquidity reporting, is highly desirable
Exposure to sustainability reporting frameworks or Finance Transformation initiatives is an advantage

What’s in it for you?

Discounted ING Health Insurance
An additional Rest Day to support your wellbeing
An IMPACT Day to volunteer on approved sustainability activity

About Us

At ING, we want to make life simpler and more worthwhile – for everyone who banks with us, for the people who work with us, and the community at large, too. 
 
When you come to work at ING, you’re joining a team where individuality isn’t just accepted, it’s encouraged. We’ve built a culture that’s fun, friendly and supportive – it’s the kind of place where you can be yourself and make the most of whatever you have to offer. 
 
We give people the freedom to think differently, take ownership of their work, and make great things happen. We’re here to help you get ahead. And with our global network, there’s plenty of scope to take your career in new directions, perhaps even ones you’ve never considered. 
 
We are all about celebrating success and as a result we are proud to be a WGEA Employer of Choice for Gender Equality and a certified Family Inclusive workplace.  
 
Sound like the kind of place you’d feel at home? We’d love to hear from you. 
 
(One last thing, ING operates a direct talent sourcing model. So no agency introductions, please.)

About Perilya Limited

Perilya Limited is a well-established Australian mining company with a proud history and a strong international footprint. Our operations include the iconic Broken Hill Operation in New South Wales - one of the world’s great base metals mines with over 130 years of continuous production, and Coridom in the Dominican Republic, the country’s first underground mining operation, which continues to deliver high-quality zinc, lead, copper, and silver.

Together, these assets form the foundation of a business with scale, resilience, and long-term growth opportunities.

Perilya is recognised for its commitment to operational excellence, safety leadership, and environmental responsibility. We invest in our people and communities, fostering diversity and creating genuine career pathways across both corporate and operational sites. Our collaborative culture values integrity, accountability, and continuous improvement, making Perilya a workplace where talented people can thrive and contribute to a sustainable future in mining.

The Role

We are seeking an experienced Business Analyst to join our Head Office Finance team in Adelaide. Reporting directly to the Finance Director, this role is central to supporting financial performance, strategic decision making and commercial outcomes across the business.

This is a highly finance-focused Business Analyst role with strong exposure to senior leadership. You will work at the intersection of finance, operations and strategy, providing insight-driven analysis to support business performance and investment decisions.

A key aspect of this role is partnering closely with our site operations teams. While based in Head Office, you will collaborate regularly and effectively with site-based stakeholders, including the ability to work remotely with operational teams to support informed, practical decision making.

Key responsibilities include:

Delivering detailed financial and commercial analysis to support executive decision making

Supporting budgeting, forecasting, long range planning and cost control processes

Developing and maintaining robust financial models, scenarios and sensitivities

Providing variance analysis, trend analysis and performance commentary

Supporting capital expenditure analysis, business cases and investment proposals

Enhancing management reporting, dashboards and financial metrics

Partnering closely with site operations and corporate stakeholders to improve financial and commercial outcomes

Collaborating effectively with site-based teams in a combination of remote and operationally focused environments

Driving continuous improvement in finance processes, systems and data quality

About You
You are a financially and commercially astute analyst with a strong finance foundation and the ability to influence outcomes through data, insight and collaboration with operational teams.

To succeed in this role, you will bring:

5–7 years’ experience in a Business Analyst, Commercial Analyst or Finance Analyst role

Strong background in financial analysis, modelling and reporting

Experience within mining, resources, manufacturing or capital‑intensive environments (highly regarded)

Advanced Excel skills and experience with ERP systems and BI/reporting tools (e.g. Power BI)

A solid understanding of budgeting, forecasting and financial controls

Strong stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to work collaboratively with site-based teams, including in remote or geographically dispersed environments

Confidence working with senior leaders and operational stakeholders alike

Tertiary qualification in Finance, Accounting, Economics or a related discipline

CA/CPA qualification (or progress toward) is desirable

Why Join Perilya?

Adelaide-based head office role with exposure to executive leadership

Opportunity to influence business performance in a critical industry

Collaborative, professional finance team environment

Long-term career development within an established mining company

How to Apply

To be considered for this role, please submit a brief covering letter and CV by following the APPLY NOW prompts.

Job Title: Head of Finance- FP&A and Transformation
Ad Text:
We are seeking a commercially astute and technically strong finance leader to step into a unique and high-impact role that blends FP&A leadership with "hands on" data, BI and finance transformation ownership.

This is not a traditional Head of Finance role. You will lead the finance function while also driving a Finance AI and automation agenda, building a modern, data-driven capability that fundamentally reshapes how finance operates and supports the business.

About the Role:

Reporting to the CFO, this role sits at the intersection of finance, data and technology, with accountability across three core pillars:

FP&A Leadership- owning budgeting, forecasting, reporting and strategic financial insight
Finance Transformation- delivering automation, AI enablement and a single source of truth
Billing & Claims Optimisation- overseeing a shift to a near fully automated, exception-based model




You will lead a small but highly capable team and work closely with Technology, Operations and Executive Leadership to embed data-led decision making across the organisation.




Key Responsibilities:

FP&A & Commercial Insight

Own the full FP&A function including budgets, forecasts and long range planning
Deliver high quality month-end reporting, variance analysis and performance insights
Partner with senior stakeholders to support strategic decisions and growth initiatives
Lead financial modelling, scenario analysis and M&A support




Finance AI & Transformation

Drive the implementation of finance automation and AI-enabled processes
Build and own a single source of truth through scalable dashboards and data models
Replace manual, spreadsheet driven processes with automated, system led solutions
Define data governance, structures and reporting standards




Data, BI & Systems Leadership

Work "hands on" with SQL, Python and BI tools to build reporting and analytics capability
Lead the design of dashboards, data pipelines and reporting frameworks
Collaborate with technology teams on system integration and data architecture




About You

You are a rare blend of finance leader and "hands on" data practitioner, comfortable moving between strategic thinking and technical execution.

10+ years in FP&A or commercial finance
Strong experience in financial modelling, reporting and business partnering
Capability in SQL or Python or BI tools (Power BI, Tableau or similar)
Proven track record in finance transformation, automation or systems uplift
Experience designing data models, dashboards and reporting frameworks
Strong stakeholder engagement skills, including executive and board level communication
Experience in M&A, or exposure to high growth / transformation environments (desirable)




Why This Role Stands Out

Opportunity to build a modern finance function from the ground up
High visibility and impact across executive leadership
"Hands on" influence over data, systems and strategic direction
Lead a shift to exception-based, automated finance operations




What Success Looks Like

A fully embedded FP&A cadence delivering clear, actionable insight
Finance processes significantly automated and scalable
A trusted single source of truth for financial and operational reporting
Finance recognised as a strategic partner to the business
Measurable improvements in efficiency, accuracy and decision-making capability




You must hold full, unrestricted working rights in Australia on a permanent basis.

Job Title: Senior Data & Insights Analyst (12 m contract)
Ad Text:
About the Opportunity

As Flight Centre Travel Group’s (FCTG) Senior Data & Insights Analyst you will drive how data is leveraged in solving business problems within the dynamic Leisure travel industry. You will consult with stakeholders in problem-definition, setting success metrics and shaping the way forward through data insights, analytics and effective communication with your audience.

You will use your deep practical understanding of the data, workflows, methods and tooling to autonomously drive analysis and reporting within your scope. You can adapt your tooling and approach based on business and user needs.

This involves reporting, visualisation and taking ownership of workflows and structures to make solutions scalable, reproducible, and long-term focused.

You will also collaborate with peers in data engineering, insights, analytics & data science and AI, supporting initiatives for data quality and stability, using your experience to think of scalable solutions.

This full time Brisbane-based role is for an initial 12-month contract to cover parental leave.

 

What you will do:

Extract data from multiple data sources and combine them into the required datasets for model building or analytics
Conduct exploratory data profiling techniques, product crosstabs, common summary descriptive statistics, etc.
Pick analytically valid approaches, appropriate in terms of level of effort, favouring iterative delivery that solve for the objective, not the ask
Demonstrate understanding of business domain and can recommend model changes, continuously evolving information and requirements
Become knowledgeable in visualisation tools (Power BI), creating complex calculations using advanced features
Clearly and confidently articulate decision-making rationale, solutions, methodologies and frameworks to team members and both technical and non-technical partners
Leverage data to shape your product squad's strategy, providing support and/or leadership wherever needed
Play a critical role in attacking opaque, unstructured problems; develop core analytic frameworks that shape the way the company thinks about our business
Own projects that span disparate groups with varying levels of technical fluency from end-to-end
Build out data availability and business intelligence solutions for your team
Regularly present high-level narrative-driven analyses to all levels of management across the organisation
Build a data-driven culture wherever you turn, from working with PMs on KPIs, to partnering with engineers on data models, to making sure that all decisions accurately utilise the most powerful data available
Mentor data team members in articulating ideas and viewpoints, managing resources, and driving the resolution of issues
Perform exploratory analyses and in-depth research on large data sets to uncover, size and advocate for opportunities that improve the customer experience and key growth KPIs like customer conversion and retention
Communicate not just findings, but also your recommendations to key stakeholders and leadership - you will use your insights to advocate for our customers
Partner closely with stakeholders across various teams to ensure that priorities and resources are aligned

 

What you will need:

5+ years of experience in product and customer analytics
3+ years of full-time working experience in quantitative analysis roles
Experience using relational databases and big data via writing performant queries in SQL against multi-terabyte and billion row datasets in Azure Data Lake, EDW, AWS or Hadoop environments
Experience with modern web analytics tools (Adobe analytics, Google Analytics, Mixpanel, etc.)
Experience using modern visualisation tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker, Qlik)
Solid experience hiring, building, mentoring and leading a team
Experience in a relevant area – travel, E-commerce, or technology is a strong plus
Excellent oral and written skills to communicate complex issues and influence others internally and externally
Strong interpersonal skills and ability to gain the respect of senior management and external executives and partners
Excellent quantitative, analytical and critical thinking skills
Ability to work and excel in a fast-paced and fluid environment
Bachelors or Masters in Mathematics, Data Science or Analytics, or other numerate, finance, business degree

 

What you will enjoy:

💰Salary: The successful candidate will be rewarded with a generous base salary package up to $140 k (+ super), based on experience

🙋🏾‍♀️Culture: you will be employed by an inclusive company that offers a team culture like no other in the industry - Flight Centre Travel Group Australia is proud to be a certified Great Place To Work® Australia New Zealand Pty Ltd

👐🏽 Our People: FCTG is an Equal Opportunity Employer and encourage all suitably qualified applicants to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander People and people from racial, ethnic, or ethno-religious minorities and people with disability, regardless of race, colour, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status 

🎉Development: Individualised Learning & Development pathway options

🔧Access to 'LinkedIn Learning' for ongoing skills development: over 24,500 courses in over 20 languages

🏆Exclusive Staff Discounts: Accessible via our employee-only portal with 500+ of Australia’s leading retailers, health and wellness discounts, financial planning advice, employee share plan and more

🌏Travel Discounts: Including family and friends - on flights, hotels, tours, cruises, travel insurance and more

💎Brightness of Future: Career opportunities in a network of brands and businesses across the globe - we promote from within  

🥦Corporate Health Discounts: Access exclusive discounts on health insurance plans for you and your family with our partner, BUPA, access to internal gym

🌞Mental Health: Support and Employee Assistance Program for staff and family

🎉Social: Embrace the famous Flight Centre culture at our Reward & Recognition events throughout the year including our most popular events on the calendar Global Gathering (Bangkok in 2026) and EOFY Celebration  

🙏🏼 Giving Back: Corporate Social Responsibility program supporting nominated charities through Workplace Giving, volunteering and fundraising; paid parental and volunteer leave

💚Sustainability: The protection of our environment is essential to the future of tourism, as a leading travel group, we have made efforts to affect positive change to the environment

 

Please note: applications can only be considered by candidates who are based, and have the right to work, in Australia without restriction or requiring visa sponsorship. Remote working options are not available for this position.

Job Title: Consultant / Senior Consultant - Corporate Finance / Advisory (Healthcare)
Category: Corporate Finance & Investment Banking (Banking & Financial Services)
Ad Text:
About Paxon Consulting Group

Paxon is a leading advisory firm providing financial, commercial and strategic advice to support clients in making critical business decisions – specialising in the design and delivery of innovative commercial and financial solutions for major infrastructure and service redesign projects across the Asia Pacific region and the Middle East.

Our people are innovative, creative and experts in delivering advice that drives value for our clients, for the sectors in which they operate, and for the broader economy.

Paxon’s dedicated health-sector team works closely with senior leaders and decision-makers in public and private healthcare organisations at every level of the healthcare ecosystem, across Australia and in the Middle East. Each year Paxon advises on over $5 billion of new hospital infrastructure, influences the development of models of care and the way that innovation and technology can assist.  We also advise on specialist hospital services including pharmacy, medical imaging, rehabilitation, chemotherapy and dialysis.

We provide robust analysis and decision-making support, to help our clients make critical decisions about how healthcare is delivered. We often operate at the intersection of public and private sectors, helping our clients to create an effective, value-driven healthcare system.

The types of strategic decisions we typically support clients with include:

·          Business case and feasibility studies for health care programs, projects and assets

·          Financing for infrastructure investments

·          Mergers and acquisition / divestment of hospital and health assets

·          Collaborative partnerships between Governments and the private sector

·          Service outsourcing and procurement

·          Service reconfiguration and transitions

·          Costing and modelling of new business models

·          New model of care development

·          Evaluation and redesign of health programs

Beyond healthcare, Paxon has market-leading expertise in many other social infrastructure sectors, including education, housing, real estate, urban renewal and land activation, corrections, sport and recreation, renewable energy, water, ports and airports. Paxon’s core strengths are in financial, transaction and PPP (public private partnerships) advisory for social infrastructure projects.  We’ve advised on over US$130 billion of projects over the last 20 years.

The Role

Due to continued growth, Paxon is seeking Consultants or Senior Consultants to join our team in both our Perth and Sydney offices. This is the ideal next step for someone with prior experience in financial modelling in an advisory field, who is wanting to retain their technical skills, but progress further into commercial and financial advisory support.

As part of our team, you will have the opportunity to engage with healthcare clients and stakeholders across Australia, Asia Pacific and the Middle East, providing expert advice and leadership in various aspects of health system development and financing, including:

Collaborate closely with clients to understand their specific objectives and challenges

Undertake research, financial modelling and risk assessment for a range of healthcare infrastructure and service redesign projects

Commercial structuring of projects and healthcare investments, including development of commercial and financial structures and contracting arrangements

Assess the financial, operational and strategic dimensions of projects and policies

Prepare clear, compelling reports, recommendations and presentations for clients and stakeholders

The financial and commercial analysis of the impact and effectiveness of various healthcare projects, facilitating informed decision-making processes

This is the ideal role for you if you:

·         Have several years’ relevant experience in advisory, banking or corporate organisations and have a good grounding that has honed your commercial thinking, communication and technical skills

·         Have since realised that you want to work in Project or Corporate Finance in an advisory capacity and accelerate your career by working on high-profile, meaningful projects

·         Have experience in the health care sector

·         Have a finance / commerce background backed by studies in mathematics, data science, engineering or finance

·         Are proud to be known for your excellent Excel skills, commercial acumen and exceptional communication and presentation skills

·         Enjoy solving complex problems effectively through the use of your research and analytical skills

·         Are prepared to take ownership for your work and professional development and ask questions, rather than waiting for the answers to be given to you

·         Are looking for somewhere that you can grow your career over the long-term whilst working with smart people on interesting projects that have a social purpose.

Applications

This is a great opportunity to join a team embarking on an exciting journey of growth, contributing to leading social infrastructure projects that will positively impact millions of people.

If you possess the skills and experience outlined above, we encourage you to apply by submitting your resume and cover letter.

Ad Text:
The Senior Manager, Enterprise Applications, is responsible for the strategic direction, development, and governance of QRIDA's core software assets. Define the technical standards, tools, and methodologies (e.g., Agile, Scrum) used across the development lifecycle.

For a full description of the key responsibilities, please refer to the role description.

Joining our passionate team means an opportunity to learn, further your career, have your ideas heard and make a difference for rural and regional Queenslanders every day.

Staff have access to:

Salary Packaging, 12.75% super and leave loading

Flexible working arrangements including balancing of in office/working from home arrangements

Career and Performance Development Planning with the focus on setting of goals and career development

Learning & Development including access to the QRIDA Aspire Scheme

Employee assistance and benefits programs including access to Rewards Gateway (after meeting eligibility requirements)

QRIDA is a small organisation of just over two hundred employees. We value diversity in our community and work hard to foster an environment of inclusivity and trust.

The Queensland Rural and Industry Development Authority (QRIDA) is a specialist provider of government financial and advisory support to rural and regional Queensland.

As a statutory body of the Queensland Government we have been helping primary producers and industry recover from natural disaster, improve sustainability and profitability, make a start in business, and overcome difficult conditions, for over 30 years.

As the Queensland Government's experts in rural finance, we also administer the State Government's Farm Business Debt Mediation program, the Farm Debt Reconstruction Office, conduct a biennial Rural Debt Survey, undertake research and provide advice to the Minister for Agricultural Industry Development and Fisheries and Minister for Rural Communities.

QRIDA is empowered under the Rural and Regional Adjustment Act 1994 to deliver additional programs and services across a range of industry sectors and to local, state and territory governments throughout Australia and for the Australian Government.

We're committed to do more to be a proactive, responsible and reliable provider of financial management services and assistance to create sustainable rural communities.

Delivery. Assurance. Real Outcomes

OUR CLIENT

Our client is a well-renowned public health organisation located in Melbourne’s Eastern Suburbs. The Procurement function operates within a collaborative, values-driven environment focused on delivering strong commercial outcomes while ensuring compliance with Victorian Government procurement policies and frameworks.

With an increasing portfolio of capital projects and organisational transformation initiatives, the business is seeking a Procurement Process & Governance Manager to lead procurement excellence, governance uplift and support the delivery of complex procurement activities across Capital Projects including Construction, Refurbishments, FFE and Medical Equipment.

ROLE

As the Procurement Process & Governance Manager, you will play a critical role in strengthening procurement frameworks, policies and processes across the organisation. You will work closely with the GM Procurement and senior stakeholders to ensure procurement activities are compliant, efficient, and aligned with Victorian Government requirements while enabling effective service delivery outcomes for patients.

This role will see you driving governance improvements and supporting high-priority procurement initiatives beyond the day-to-day pipeline. You will engage broadly across the organisation, providing expert advice, uplifting capability and ensuring best practice procurement standards are embedded.
Projects within scope will range from governance and policy uplift through to supporting complex sourcing activities, including high-value and high-risk procurement processes requiring strong oversight and stakeholder engagement.

Specifically, you will:

Lead the development, implementation and continuous improvement of procurement policies, frameworks and governance processes
• Ensure compliance with Victorian Government procurement regulations, audit requirements and internal controls
• Provide expert procurement advice to senior stakeholders on governance, risk and probity matters
• Drive procurement capability uplift across the organisation through training, tools and process improvements
• Build strong internal relationships to ensure alignment between procurement strategy and business objectives
SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

The successful candidate will bring strong experience in procurement governance, process improvement and complex sourcing within regulated environments. You will be comfortable working with senior stakeholders, operating autonomously while contributing to broader strategic objectives led by the GM Procurement.

You are a structured and commercially astute professional who can balance governance requirements with practical delivery outcomes. Experience within public sector or health is highly regarded, particularly with an understanding of Victorian Government procurement frameworks.

5+ years’ experience in Procurement, with a focus on governance, process or category management
• Strong knowledge of public sector procurement frameworks (Victorian Government experience highly desirable)
• Proven ability to design and implement procurement policies, processes and controls
• High attention to detail with strong risk management and compliance capability
• Strong stakeholder engagement and influencing skills
• Ability to operate autonomously while managing multiple priorities
GET IN TOUCH

If you require more information on the role or if you have any further questions, please contact Rory O’Sullivan via email at rory@loop-recruit.com

Our Approach to Flexible Work
 

With Flex Work, we’re combining the best of both worlds: in-person time and remote. Our approach enables our teams to deepen connections, maintain a strong community, and do their best work. We know that flexibility can take shape in many ways, so rather than a number of required days in-office each week, we simply spend at least half (50%) of our time each quarter in the office or in the field with our customers, prospects, and partners (depending on role). This means you'll have the freedom to create a flexible schedule that caters to your business, team, and personal needs, while being intentional to make the most of time spent together. Those in our remote "home office" roles also have the opportunity to come together in our offices for important moments that matter.
